preamble: The shift between the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] and the voiceless velar fricative [x] is a common form of fronting and devoicing, frequently observed in the development of 'guttural' rhotics and back fricatives in Germanic and Romance languages.

examples.note: Georgian has both ხ [x] (velar) and uvular sounds. Some Proto-Kartvelian uvular fricatives shifted to velar [x] in Georgian daughter languages through fronting of the uvular place of articulation.
Widespread shift where the uvular rhotic is realized as a voiceless velar fricative in many dialects (e.g., Rio de Janeiro).
Correspondence between Northern uvular realizations and Southern velar realizations of the 'hard G'.
